questions.text: In the context of ISO 27001:2022, what is the correct relationship between risk assessment and risk treatment?
options: C. Risk assessment and risk treatment are identical processes performed simultaneously
D. Risk assessment is optional if risk treatment controls already exist
B. Risk treatment must precede risk assessment to establish baseline controls
A. Risk assessment identifies and analyzes risks; risk treatment determines how to address identified risks
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1 and 6.2 establish that risk assessment (Annex A) precedes risk treatment decisions. Assessment analyzes probability and impact; treatment selects appropriate responses (mitigate, avoid, transfer, accept).
questions.justification_wrong: Risk treatment requires prior assessment results; they are sequential, not simultaneous or interchangeable. Assessment is mandatory for informed treatment decisions.

questions.text: Which control domain from ISO 27002:2022 addresses the requirement to define roles, responsibilities, and segregation of duties?
options: A. Organizational controls (A.5)
B. People controls (A.6)
C. Physical controls (A.7)
D. Technological controls (A.8)
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Section A.5 (Organizational) includes controls 5.1-5.23 covering organizational structure, governance, policies, and role definitions. Control 5.3 specifically addresses segregation of duties and conflict management.
questions.justification_wrong: People controls address competence and awareness; physical controls address facilities; technological controls address systems. Role definition is an organizational governance function.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022, what is the significance of the 'Plan-Do-Check-Act' (PDCA) cycle in ISMS management?
options: C. It ensures all controls are applied simultaneously without prioritization
B. It is a one-time implementation model applied only during initial ISMS deployment
A. It provides a structured, continuous improvement framework for ISMS implementation, maintenance, and optimization
D. It eliminates the need for periodic management reviews and audits
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Section 4.4 emphasizes PDCA as foundational for continuous ISMS improvement. Plan (strategy/objectives), Do (implement), Check (monitor/audit), Act (correct/improve) ensures systematic, iterative enhancement.
questions.justification_wrong: PDCA is continuous, not one-time. It accommodates phased implementation and does not negate audits—it mandates them as part of Check phase.

questions.text: Which of the following statements accurately reflects the relationship between ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls and organizational context?
options: B. Organizations select and implement controls from Annex A based on risk assessment results and organizational context
C. Annex A controls are recommendations only and do not require formal implementation decisions
D. Organizations may ignore Annex A if they develop equivalent proprietary security controls
A. All 93 Annex A controls must be implemented identically across all organizations without customization
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 requires organizations to determine control objectives and controls using Annex A as reference, selecting based on risk assessment, legal requirements, and organizational context. Implementation tailoring is essential.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls must be contextualized per Clause 4.1 (organizational objectives); universal identical implementation contradicts risk-based approach. Annex A is normative reference, not optional.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27002:2022, which control category directly addresses the establishment of information security policies and governance structures?
options: B. People controls (Theme 2) managing employee responsibilities
D. Technological controls (Theme 4) implementing encryption mechanisms
A. Organizational controls (Theme 1) establishing policies and governance frameworks
C. Physical controls (Theme 3) securing facility access
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Theme 1 (Organizational - 37 controls) encompasses governance, policies, and strategic direction. Controls like A.5.1, A.6.1 establish information security frameworks and policies fundamental to ISMS implementation.
questions.justification_wrong: Themes 2, 3, 4 support but don't directly address governance structures. People controls focus on competence/awareness; Physical controls address perimeter security; Technological controls handle technical implementations.

questions.text: In ISO 27001:2022, the concept of 'information security risk' is primarily managed through which clause and associated process?
options: D. Clause 8 (Operation) executing day-to-day risk monitoring
C. Clause 7 (Support) providing resources for risk management
B. Clause 6 (Planning) through risk assessment and treatment planning
A. Clause 4 (Context of the organization) defining risk criteria
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1 and 6.2 mandate risk assessment and risk treatment. The organization shall define risk criteria, assess information security risks, and plan treatments—core planning phase responsibilities.
questions.justification_wrong: Clause 4 defines context, not risk processes. Clause 7 provides enablers (resources, competence). Clause 8 executes controls but doesn't establish risk treatment planning framework.

questions.text: Which ISO 27002:2022 control is most critical for ensuring that personnel understand their security obligations and potential consequences of non-compliance?
options: A. A.5.2 - Information security in project management
B. A.6.2 - Information security awareness, education and training
C. A.7.1 - Cryptographic controls for data protection
D. A.8.1 - User access provisioning procedures
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Control A.6.2 (People Theme) mandates awareness and training programs to ensure all personnel understand security responsibilities, consequences of violations, and best practices—essential for human-centered security.
questions.justification_wrong: A.5.2 addresses project governance; A.7.1 is cryptographic technology; A.8.1 handles access provisioning. None directly target awareness and accountability understanding as A.6.2 does.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 8.2, what is the primary purpose of information security event monitoring and response procedures?
options: D. To eliminate all security vulnerabilities permanently
A. To establish baseline security metrics for annual reporting
B. To detect, respond to, and learn from security incidents and anomalies
C. To replace backup and recovery procedures
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.2 requires detection, response, and post-incident analysis of security events. Organizations must monitor for anomalies, respond promptly, and extract lessons learned to improve controls.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A addresses reporting (Clause 9), not response procedures. Option C overstates scope—incident response complements but doesn't replace BC/DR. Option D is unrealistic; response manages incidents, not prevents all vulnerabilities.

questions.text: In ISO 27002:2022, which technological control theme specifically addresses the protection of information in transit and at rest across networks and storage systems?
options: A. A.5.12 - Management of removable media
D. A.6.5 - Access control policy enforcement
C. A.8.2 - Privileged access management
B. A.7.10 - Information transfer (encryption and secure channels)
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Control A.7.10 (Technological Theme) mandates encryption for data in transit via secure channels and protocols. Complements A.7.2 (encryption at rest) for comprehensive cryptographic protection across lifecycle.
questions.justification_wrong: A.5.12 addresses physical media handling; A.8.2 focuses on privileged user access; A.6.5 enforces access policies. None directly mandate encryption/secure channels for information transfer as A.7.10 does.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 implementation, you must establish roles and responsibilities for information security. Which control from ISO 27002:2022 specifically addresses the organizational structure and assignment of information security responsibilities?
options: A. Control 5.1 - Policies for information security
C. Control 5.3 - Allocation of information security responsibilities
B. Control 6.1 - Organization and management of information security
D. Control 7.1 - Human resources security roles
questions.justification_correct: Control 6.1 addresses organizational structure, establishing roles, and defining responsibility distribution for information security management. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5.3 requires this organizational framework for effective governance implementation.
questions.justification_wrong: While 5.1 covers policies, 5.3 addresses allocation but not organizational structure comprehensively. Control 7.1 focuses on HR security, not governance organization per ISO 27002:2022 Section A.6.

questions.text: An organization implements supplier management controls under ISO 27001:2022. Which statement correctly reflects the primary objective of Control 5.19 (Supplier relationships) regarding information security?
options: B. Establish requirements for information security in supplier contracts and monitor compliance
D. Replace internal controls with supplier-provided security measures
C. Transfer all security responsibilities exclusively to external suppliers
A. Eliminate all third-party involvement in information processing
questions.justification_correct: Control 5.19 requires establishing contractual information security requirements with suppliers and monitoring their compliance. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.2 mandates managing information security risks from suppliers through documented agreements.
questions.justification_wrong: Eliminating third-party involvement is impractical. Transferring all responsibility removes organizational accountability. Replacing internal controls contradicts the defense-in-depth principle required by ISO 27001:2022.

questions.text: When implementing access control measures per ISO 27002:2022 Control 8.2 (User access management), which element is NOT a core component of user access provisioning?
options: C. Implementation of multi-factor authentication on all systems
A. Formal user registration and de-registration procedures
D. Documentation of approved access rights and access restrictions
B. Review and authorization of access requests before granting
questions.justification_correct: While multi-factor authentication is important, it is an authentication mechanism (Control 8.3) rather than a core user access management component. Control 8.2 focuses on provisioning processes: registration, authorization, documentation, and de-registration.
questions.justification_wrong: All other options directly address user access provisioning requirements in ISO 27002:2022 Control 8.2, including formal procedures, authorization workflows, and documented access rights tracking.

questions.text: An organization's incident management process under ISO 27001:2022 must include specific elements. Which control describes the requirement to assess incidents and determine if they constitute information security events?
options: D. Control 5.23 - Information security incident management procedures
A. Control 8.16 - Event logging and monitoring
C. Control 8.14 - Monitoring, logging, and time synchronization
B. Control 8.15 - Detection and response to information security incidents
questions.justification_correct: Control 8.15 specifically requires organizations to assess all reported incidents to determine if they are information security events, then respond appropriately. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.4 mandates incident assessment and response procedures.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 8.16 focuses on logging. 8.14 addresses monitoring and time synchronization. 5.23 covers procedural frameworks but not the specific assessment determination required by Control 8.15.

questions.text: During a risk assessment for ISO 27001:2022 compliance, you identify that physical security controls are inadequately implemented. Which organizational control should be established FIRST to address gaps in physical security governance before implementing technical controls?
options: A. Control 4.1 - Determine scope and boundaries of the ISMS
B. Control 5.2 - Information security roles and responsibilities assignment
C. Control 5.15 - Coordination of security controls across the organization
D. Control 6.2 - Mobile devices and teleworking security policy
questions.justification_correct: Control 5.15 coordinates implementation of security controls organization-wide, establishing governance before deploying physical controls. ISO 27001:2022 requires governance framework before operational control implementation per Clause 5 requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: 4.1 addresses scope definition. 5.2 covers role assignment but not coordination. 6.2 addresses specific mobile/telework policies. None precedes comprehensive physical security governance like 5.15 coordination mandate.

questions.text: According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control objective is fundamental to establishing an Information Security Management System (ISMS) at the organizational level?
options: B. Installing firewalls and intrusion detection systems across all network segments
C. Implementing biometric authentication for all user access points
A. Defining policies, objectives, and responsibility assignments for information security governance
D. Conducting quarterly penetration testing of external networks
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 5.1-5.2 requires organizations establish policies, assign roles, and define objectives. Governance provides the foundation for all subsequent controls across organizational, people, physical, and technological domains.
questions.justification_wrong: Technical controls (B, C, D) are important but cannot substitute foundational governance. Without organizational-level policy and accountability structures, technical implementations lack strategic alignment and accountability frameworks required by 27001:2022.

questions.text: In the context of ISO/IEC 27002:2022 people-centric controls, what is the primary objective of implementing competence assessment and training programs for information security personnel?
options: C. To replace technical controls with awareness-raising initiatives across the organization
D. To establish a hierarchical certification system determining employee career progression
A. To ensure personnel possess necessary knowledge and skills to execute security responsibilities and adapt to evolving threats
B. To create mandatory annual compliance certifications for all employees without exception
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control A.6.2 emphasizes competence development enabling personnel to execute security roles effectively. Training addresses threat evolution, reducing human-factor vulnerabilities and improving control implementation effectiveness.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B and D represent compliance theater rather than competence-building. Option C incorrectly positions people controls as replacements for technical controls; they are complementary. Effective ISMS requires integrated approach across all domains per 27001:2022 clause 4.4.

questions.text: Which physical security control implementation strategy best aligns with ISO/IEC 27002:2022 requirements for protecting assets in facilities with mixed public and restricted areas?
options: B. Installing identical access controls uniformly across all facility areas regardless of asset classification
A. Implementing layered access controls with defined perimeters, entry points, and progressive security zones based on asset sensitivity
D. Implementing biometric systems only at the primary entrance while maintaining open access to server rooms
C. Relying exclusively on video surveillance systems to monitor all facility movements continuously
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 controls A.11.1-11.2 mandate perimeter security and access control based on asset criticality. Layered approaches (zones, entry points, graduated access) reduce unauthorized access risk effectively across diverse facility environments.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B and D violate principle of proportionate controls—critical assets require enhanced protection. Option C surveillance-only approach lacks preventive controls needed per A.11.2. Effective physical security integrates preventive, detective, and recovery mechanisms.

questions.text: When implementing ISO/IEC 27002:2022 technological controls for cryptography, what critical factor should drive the selection and deployment strategy?
options: D. Selecting cryptographic solutions based primarily on vendor marketing claims and cost minimization
B. Adopting the strongest encryption algorithms available regardless of operational impact or system compatibility
A. Risk assessment outcomes determining data sensitivity, threat models, regulatory requirements, and system architecture constraints
C. Implementing encryption uniformly across all systems to simplify management and compliance audits
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control A.10.1 requires cryptography decisions grounded in risk assessment. Effectiveness depends on aligning encryption strength, algorithms, and key management with assessed threats, data classification, and operational context per 27001:2022 clause 6.1.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B and C ignore context-specific security needs—inappropriate over-encryption creates operational burden and may weaken security. Option D neglects technical due diligence. Cryptography strategies require systematic risk-driven approach integrating technical, operational, and business factors.

questions.text: According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022, how should an organization approach the periodic review and update of its information security controls portfolio across all four control domains?
options: C. Updating controls only when external auditors or regulators identify specific compliance gaps or violations

A. Conducting regular reviews triggered by risk assessments, incident investigations, and environmental changes, validating control effectiveness across organizational, people, physical, and technological domains
B. Performing annual reviews of only technological controls, as organizational controls remain static once established
D. Implementing new controls exclusively when new threats emerge, without systematic evaluation of existing control performance
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 8.6 mandates periodic evaluation, and clause 10.3 requires continual improvement. Reviews must be risk-driven, incident-informed, and cover all control domains systematically. Environmental changes (threats, regulations, technology) necessitate adaptive control strategies.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B incorrectly assumes organizational controls stability—governance must evolve with organizational changes. Option C delays proactive improvement. Option D lacks systematic oversight required by PDCA cycle (Plan-Do-Check-Act). Effective ISMS requires integrated, continuous control optimization.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27002:2022, which organizational control establishes the foundation for implementing an Information Security Management System (ISMS) by defining roles, responsibilities, and accountability structures?
options: C. Control 7.1 (Personnel screening) - Addresses recruitment procedures but does not define organizational governance or accountability structures.

A. Control 5.1 (Policies for information security) - Establishes documented policies defining roles, responsibilities, and accountability mechanisms as mandatory foundation for ISMS implementation per clause 5.2 of ISO 27001:2022.
B. Control 6.1 (Internal organization) - Focuses solely on internal communication channels without establishing formal governance structures required for ISMS.
D. Control 8.1 (Physical perimeter) - Concentrates on physical security boundaries unrelated to organizational governance frameworks.
questions.justification_correct: Control 5.1 requires documented information security policies establishing roles, responsibilities, and accountability structures essential for ISMS foundation, directly supporting ISO 27001:2022 clause 5.2 governance requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 6.1 addresses internal communication; Control 7.1 covers recruitment; Control 8.1 focuses on physical security—none establish comprehensive organizational governance structures.

questions.text: In risk assessment and treatment planning under ISO 27001:2022, which approach ensures that residual risks are explicitly documented and that stakeholders understand the organization's risk acceptance rationale?
options: D. Establishing a risk register without defining residual risk thresholds or communicating acceptance criteria to organizational leadership.

A. Implementing risk response strategies and documenting risk treatment plans with clear justification of accepted residual risks per Annex A control 5.7, ensuring stakeholder awareness through information sharing protocols.
B. Conducting annual vulnerability scans only without documenting risk acceptance or justifying residual risk levels to stakeholders.
C. Relying solely on third-party penetration testing reports as evidence of risk mitigation without formal risk treatment documentation.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clauses 6.1-6.2 require explicit documentation of risk treatment plans, residual risks, and acceptance justifications. Control 5.7 (Threat intelligence) supports informed risk decisions through stakeholder communication.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B-D lack formal documentation, stakeholder engagement, and explicit residual risk acceptance required by ISO 27001:2022 for demonstrable risk governance.

questions.text: Which combination of ISO 27002:2022 people controls ensures comprehensive protection against insider threats while maintaining employee trust and psychological safety?
options: B. Controls 7.2 (Induction, transfer, termination), 7.3 (Responsibilities), 8.5 (Access control) combined with reporting mechanisms and awareness programs establishing accountability while protecting psychological safety.
D. Controls 5.1 (Policies) and 6.1 (Internal organization) without defining specific personnel security measures or incident reporting frameworks.
C. Control 6.7 (Threat intelligence) alone, assuming technical intelligence prevents all insider threat vectors.
A. Controls 7.2 (Induction, transfer, termination), 7.3 (Responsibilities), and 8.2 (User endpoint devices) focusing exclusively on technical monitoring and access restrictions.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 people controls 7.2-7.3 and 8.5 establish lifecycle management, clear responsibilities, and access governance. Combined with awareness programs, they mitigate insider risks while fostering trust and accountability.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A ignores people-centric controls; Option C relies on technical intelligence only; Option D lacks specific personnel security measures required for comprehensive insider threat mitigation.

questions.text: In implementing cryptographic controls (ISO 27002:2022 Control 10.1), which decision framework balances regulatory compliance, key management complexity, and organizational risk tolerance for algorithm selection?
options: D. Deferring cryptographic decisions to external auditors without organizational participation in algorithm selection and key management planning.
C. Implementing encryption uniformly across all systems regardless of data classification or sensitivity levels, treating all data identically.

B. Establishing a decision matrix evaluating algorithm strength against regulatory mandates (GDPR, PCI-DSS), key lifecycle management capabilities, and organizational risk tolerance thresholds with documented justification.
A. Selecting cryptographic algorithms based solely on vendor recommendations and industry popularity without assessing regulatory requirements or organizational risk appetite.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Control 10.1 requires cryptographic controls aligned with regulatory obligations and organizational context. A structured decision matrix ensures compliance, manageability, and risk alignment per ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.2.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A ignores compliance requirements; Option C disregards risk-based data classification; Option D abdicates organizational responsibility for critical security decisions per ISO 27001:2022.

questions.text: When conducting a gap analysis for ISO 27001:2022 certification readiness, which assessment methodology integrates the 93 controls across organizational, people, physical, and technological domains while identifying interdependencies?
options: C. Focusing exclusively on Annex A (37 organizational controls) while deferring people, physical, and technological controls to future assessment phases.

B. Mapping all 93 controls to organizational processes, identifying interdependencies, and assessing maturity levels with weighted risk prioritization across domains, supported by evidence documentation.
D. Utilizing automated scanning tools exclusively without qualitative assessment of control implementation effectiveness or stakeholder competence validation.
A. Domain-siloed assessments evaluating each of 4 themes independently without analyzing cross-domain control dependencies or cumulative risk impacts.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 gap analysis requires comprehensive mapping of all 93 controls, interdependency analysis, and maturity assessment across 4 domains. This holistic approach per ISO 19011:2021 ensures systematic certification readiness.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A ignores critical interdependencies; Option C defers essential controls; Option D relies on technical scanning without qualitative control effectiveness assessment required by ISO 27001:2022.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 implementation audit, you identify that the organization's ISMS documentation lacks explicit linkage between risk assessment results and selected controls. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.2, what is the primary purpose of establishing this relationship?
options: B. To ensure all 93 ISO 27002:2022 controls are implemented without exception
C. To eliminate the need for periodic risk reassessment cycles
D. To replace the Statement of Applicability with a risk register document
A. To demonstrate that controls are proportionate to identified risks and support informed decision-making on risk acceptance
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.2 requires organizations to determine and evaluate information security risks. The relationship between risks and controls ensures proportionality and informed risk treatment decisions, meeting clause 6.2.1(d) requirements for documented risk treatment.
questions.justification_wrong: While ISO 27002 provides control guidance, implementation flexibility exists. Risk reassessment remains mandatory. SoA documents control applicability decisions, not risk registers. Non-selective implementation contradicts risk-based approach principle.

questions.text: An organization implements multi-factor authentication (MFA) as a compensating control for weak password policies. According to ISO 27002:2022 5.15 and ISO 27001:2022 clause 8.3, what critical requirement must be documented to justify this substitution?
options: B. A list of all users affected by the compensating control change
D. Employee training completion certificates for MFA usage
A. A documented risk assessment demonstrating MFA effectiveness is equivalent or superior; approval from risk owner; validation evidence; and periodic review schedule
C. Vendor certification that MFA meets ISO 27002 standards
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 8.3 requires documented risk treatment. Compensating controls demand evidence of equivalent/superior risk reduction, documented approval, validation, and ongoing monitoring per ISO 27002:2022 5.15 guidance on control implementation.
questions.justification_wrong: User lists relate to implementation but not justification. Vendor certificates don't prove equivalent risk reduction. Training is operational requirement, not compensating control justification per normative clauses 8.3 and 6.2.

questions.text: During an internal audit, you discover the organization accepted residual risk from unpatched legacy systems without documented risk owner approval. Which ISO 27001:2022 clause violation is most critical, and what corrective action is mandatory?
options: C. Clause 6.1 violation: Asset inventory incomplete. Immediate action: Create comprehensive asset register before resuming operations

A. Clause 5.1 violation: Risk owner accountability missing. Immediate action: Establish risk acceptance policy with formal owner roles; document existing residual risks; obtain explicit written approvals with risk tolerance criteria
D. Clause 7.2 violation: Competence gaps. Immediate action: Hire external consultants to manage all risks
B. Clause 8.1 violation: Mandatory control selection. Immediate action: Implement all ISO 27002 controls regardless of risk levels
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 5.1 establishes leadership accountability and risk ownership. Clause 6.1.2 mandates documented risk acceptance decisions. Documented approvals with explicit risk acceptance statements and criteria are normative requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: Clause 8.1 emphasizes risk-based selection, not mandatory implementation of all controls. Clause 6.1 addresses organizational context, not primary violation. Clause 7.2 is competence-focused. Risk acceptance requires documented leadership approval per 5.1.

questions.text: An organization operating across three jurisdictions (EU-GDPR, Brazil-LGPD, Singapore-PDPA) must establish information classification and handling procedures. Per ISO 27002:2022 2.1 (Organizational Controls) and 5.13, what integrated approach minimizes compliance gaps?
options: D. Classify data by sensitivity only; allow local teams to determine regulatory handling individually

A. Develop unified classification scheme identifying common regulatory requirements; assign stricter requirements where jurisdictions overlap; document explicit mappings; implement jurisdiction-specific labels and handling procedures; conduct cross-jurisdictional impact assessments
B. Create separate ISMS instances for each jurisdiction with independent control sets
C. Implement GDPR requirements globally as the strictest baseline; eliminate jurisdiction-specific procedures
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 2.1 and 5.13 require context-aware classification. Unified scheme with jurisdiction-specific mappings ensures consistency while respecting legal variations. ISO 27001 clause 4.3 mandates external requirement determination. Cross-jurisdictional impact assessments prevent compliance blind spots.
questions.justification_wrong: Separate ISMS instances contradict integrated governance. GDPR baseline ignores jurisdiction-specific requirements (e.g., LGPD mandatory data localization). Decentralized classification creates inconsistency and auditability issues. Sensitivity-only classification misses regulatory nuances.

questions.text: During a control effectiveness assessment, you observe that cryptographic key management procedures lack defined renewal thresholds and emergency rotation procedures. Per ISO 27002:2022 5.17 and ISO 27001:2022 clause 8.4, what is the primary deficiency and required remediation?
options: D. Deficiency: Insufficient user training. Remediation: Provide annual cryptography workshops to all staff members
C. Deficiency: Missing hardware security modules (HSMs). Remediation: Immediately purchase HSM infrastructure regardless of risk assessment outcomes
B. Deficiency: Lack of encryption algorithms. Remediation: Deploy AES-256 encryption organization-wide without key management procedures

A. Deficiency: Incomplete key lifecycle management. Remediation: Document key renewal thresholds based on key age, algorithm strength, and usage patterns; establish emergency rotation procedures triggered by compromise indicators; define key escrow and recovery mechanisms; implement monitoring and audit trails
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 5.17 requires secure cryptographic key management including lifecycle, renewal, and compromise procedures. ISO 27001:2022 clause 8.4 mandates control implementation addressing identified risks. Thresholds, emergency procedures, and audit trails are key lifecycle essentials.
questions.justification_wrong: Algorithm selection without key management is incomplete. HSM implementation must follow risk assessment, not precede it. Training alone doesn't address technical control gaps. These are supporting, not primary, remediation actions per normative control requirements.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 implementation, you must establish an asset inventory covering all organizational assets. According to ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control category should primarily govern the classification and handling of sensitive data assets?
options: A. Control 5.9 - Inventory and ownership of assets
D. Control 5.5 - Access control implementation
B. Control 6.1 - Screening and background verification
C. Control 8.1 - Physical security perimeters
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.9 explicitly requires maintaining an inventory of assets with clear ownership and classification. This organizational control ensures assets are identified, documented, and appropriately protected throughout their lifecycle.
questions.justification_wrong: While 6.1 addresses personnel screening, 8.1 covers physical boundaries, and 5.5 addresses access—none directly mandate asset inventory creation and classification requirements as comprehensively as Control 5.9.

questions.text: An organization implements multi-factor authentication (MFA) for remote access. Which ISO/IEC 27002:2022 technological control specifically addresses this authentication mechanism, and at what implementation level should it be applied?
options: D. Control 5.3 - Segregation of duties in access management
A. Control 8.3 - Physical security perimeters with biometric locks
B. Control 5.15 - Authentication mechanisms for user access
C. Control 6.8 - User authentication during access provisioning
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.15 directly addresses authentication mechanisms, including multi-factor authentication. It requires implementing strong authentication for all users, especially for privileged and remote access scenarios.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 8.3 addresses physical security, 6.8 focuses on provisioning process, and 5.3 addresses segregation of duties—none specifically mandate technical authentication mechanism implementation like Control 5.15.

questions.text: Your organization experiences a data breach affecting personal data of EU residents. Which ISO 27001:2022 requirement and corresponding ISO/IEC 27002:2022 control ensure incident response procedures align with regulatory notification timelines?
options: C. Clause 8.1 with Control 8.2 - Physical access logging and retention
D. Clause 5.3 with Control 5.1 - Information security policy and governance
B. Clause 6.2 with Control 6.5 - Personnel training on breach protocols
A. Clause 8.2 with Control 5.23 - Incident response procedures and timelines
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.2 requires incident response procedures. ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.23 mandates documented procedures addressing detection, response, and communication within regulatory timeframes (72 hours for GDPR).
questions.justification_wrong: While 6.5 addresses training, 8.2 covers physical logging, and 5.1 establishes policy—none specifically mandate incident response timelines and regulatory notification procedures as Control 5.23 does.

questions.text: An organization outsources cloud infrastructure to a third-party provider. According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control framework addresses supplier risk management and contractual security requirements?
options: B. Control 6.2 - Background verification of cloud provider employees
A. Control 5.22 - Supplier relationships and information security agreements
C. Control 8.1 - Physical security controls at data center locations
D. Control 5.12 - Access rights review and privilege management
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.22 establishes requirements for supplier relationships including security assessments, contractual clauses, and oversight. It mandates information security requirements in supplier agreements and monitoring mechanisms.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 6.2 addresses personnel screening, 8.1 covers physical perimeters, and 5.12 addresses access reviews—none comprehensively establish supplier relationship governance, security assessments, and contractual frameworks.

questions.text: During a lead implementer audit, you must verify encryption implementation for data at rest and in transit. Which ISO/IEC 27002:2022 controls specifically govern cryptographic protection across different data states?
options: C. Controls 6.1 and 6.2 - Screening and training requirements
A. Controls 5.16 and 5.17 - Encryption of data at rest and in transit
B. Controls 8.2 and 8.3 - Physical security and access controls
D. Controls 5.1 and 5.2 - Policy and governance frameworks
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Controls 5.16 (data at rest) and 5.17 (data in transit) specifically mandate cryptographic protection. They address algorithm selection, key management, and implementation across system states per industry standards.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls 8.2-8.3 address physical security, 6.1-6.2 cover personnel, and 5.1-5.2 establish governance—none specifically mandate cryptographic mechanisms for different data states like Controls 5.16 and 5.17.

questions.text: An organization implements a Risk and Vulnerability Management program under ISO 27001:2022. During the annual review, you identify that vulnerability assessments are conducted only when new systems are deployed. What is the PRIMARY gap according to ISO 27002:2022 control 5.7?
options: A. Vulnerability assessments must be performed on a regular, periodic basis and when significant changes occur
C. Vulnerability assessments should be performed annually regardless of system changes
B. Vulnerability assessments are only required for critical infrastructure systems
D. Vulnerability assessments must be automated and continuous without human intervention
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control 5.7 (Information security for supplier relationships) and vulnerability management controls require regular, periodic assessments aligned with risk levels and organizational context, not just deployment events.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D misinterpret scope (not critical-only), frequency (not always annual), and automation requirements (human review essential). Risk-based approach governs assessment frequency.

questions.text: During a Lead Implementer audit, you discover that an organization's access control policy does not explicitly address the principle of least privilege in role-based assignment. Which ISO 27002:2022 control is DIRECTLY violated, and what is the remediation priority?
options: A. Control 5.15 (Access Control); remediation is HIGH priority due to foundational security impact
B. Control 6.2 (Personnel Security); remediation is MEDIUM priority affecting HR processes only
C. Control 5.3 (Segregation of Duties); remediation is LOW priority if monitoring exists
D. Control 8.1 (User Endpoint Devices); remediation is HIGH but only for remote workers
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control 5.15 (Access Control) explicitly mandates least privilege in role-based design. This is foundational—violation directly enables unauthorized access, privilege escalation, and insider threats.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B confuses scope; 6.2 covers background checks, not role design. Option C (5.3) addresses segregation but not role granularity. Option D limits to endpoints incorrectly.

questions.text: An organization's Incident Response Plan includes procedures for evidence preservation and forensic analysis. However, the plan lacks defined roles for incident communication with external authorities and regulatory bodies. Under ISO 27001:2022, which control area and compliance risk does this represent?
options: D. Control 8.16 (Monitoring Activities); MEDIUM risk limited to audit trail completeness
C. Control 5.33 (Information Security Continuity); LOW risk if internal incident response is robust
B. Control 6.5 (Supplier Relationships); MEDIUM risk affecting only third-party communication protocols
A. Control 5.28 (Incident Management); HIGH risk of legal non-compliance and regulatory sanctions due to delayed reporting
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Annex A, control 5.28 (Incident Management) requires defined responsibilities for external communication. Absence creates legal exposure—many jurisdictions mandate timely notification to authorities and data subjects.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B (6.5) addresses supplier contracts, not incident authorities. Option C (5.33) covers continuity, not incident communication roles. Option D (8.16) concerns monitoring, not notification governance.

questions.text: During implementation, you audit the Cryptographic Controls framework. The organization uses AES-128 for data in transit and at rest. A risk assessment identifies potential quantum computing threats within 15 years. What is the ISO 27002:2022 compliant response?
options: C. Implement AES-128 with hardware security modules; ISO standards do not address quantum risks
D. Defer cryptographic changes until quantum-resistant algorithms are ISO-standardized (post-2030)
B. Continue AES-128; quantum threats are theoretical and do not require action per ISO 27002:2022
A. Migrate immediately to AES-256 and establish a post-quantum cryptography transition roadmap aligned with NIST standards
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control 5.10 (Cryptography) requires algorithm selection based on risk context and threat timeline. Long-term sensitivity data demands post-quantum planning now. AES-256 plus roadmap addresses foreseeable risk.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B ignores forward-looking risk management principles. Option C underestimates quantum threats. Option D creates unacceptable risk exposure for 15-year data retention; NIST PQC finalization is 2024.

questions.text: An organization operates in both EU (GDPR) and US (CCPA) jurisdictions. The ISMS audit reveals that Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) are performed only for EU processing. What is the PRIMARY control deficiency under ISO 27001:2022, and what is required?
options: C. Control 5.1 (Policies for Information Security); ISMS context must address all applicable legal frameworks; DPIAs extend to CCPA and sector-specific regulations
A. Control 5.36 (Information Security for Supplier Relationships); DPIAs must be extended to all jurisdictions and embedded in supplier agreements
D. Control 6.1 (Screening); DPIAs are personnel vetting tools, not applicable to US processing under CCPA
B. Control 5.35 (Information Security in Project Management); DPIAs are project-phase activities, not ongoing operational controls
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.3 (determining scope) mandates inclusion of all regulatory obligations. Control 5.1 requires policies reflecting context. DPIA equivalents are necessary for CCPA (risk assessments on data sales/disclosure).
questions.justification_wrong: Option A conflates supplier controls with processing scope. Option B misdefines DPIAs as project-only; they're ongoing. Option D incorrectly denies DPIA applicability to US regulation; privacy risk assessment is universal.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27002:2022, which control objective directly addresses the establishment of a comprehensive information security governance framework that defines roles, responsibilities, and accountability structures?
options: A. Organizational controls (5.1-5.23) establishing governance structures, policies, and accountability
B. People controls (6.1-6.8) focused solely on employee training and awareness
D. Technological controls (8.1-8.34) implementing cryptographic mechanisms
C. Physical controls (7.1-7.14) addressing facility access management
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5 and ISO 27002:2022 organizational controls establish governance frameworks. Control A.5.1 requires organizations to establish policies, objectives, and accountability structures for information security management.
questions.justification_wrong: While people, physical, and technological controls support governance, they address specific implementation domains rather than establishing overarching governance structures and accountability frameworks required by Clause 5.

questions.text: In the context of ISO 27002:2022 control A.5.16 (Monitoring, review and improvement of information security), what is the primary purpose of conducting periodic internal audits and management reviews?
options: B. To exclusively document evidence for external certification audits
C. To replace external auditor reviews entirely
A. To identify compliance gaps, verify control effectiveness, and drive continuous improvement of the ISMS
D. To focus only on technological control effectiveness measurements
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 and ISO 27002:2022 A.5.16 require organizations to monitor, review, and continuously improve ISMS through internal audits and management reviews to ensure ongoing effectiveness and compliance.
questions.justification_wrong: Internal audits serve broader ISMS improvement purposes beyond certification evidence gathering. They cannot replace external audits, and must assess all control types, not only technological controls.

questions.text: According to ISO 27002:2022, which control from the People domain is essential for reducing human-related information security risks and establishing a security-conscious organizational culture?
options: A. A.6.1 (Screening) and A.6.2 (Terms and conditions of employment) establishing accountability from recruitment
B. A.7.3 (Physical access control) managing facility entry points
D. A.5.8 (Information security in supplier relationships) managing third-party risks
C. A.8.15 (Access control) implementing authentication mechanisms
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 people controls A.6.1 and A.6.2 address recruitment and employment terms, establishing security foundations. Control A.6.3 (Information security awareness, education and training) further develops security consciousness across the organization.
questions.justification_wrong: Physical controls (A.7.3) and technological controls (A.8.15) address access management; A.5.8 addresses supplier relationships. While important, these do not primarily address human-related risk reduction and culture development like people controls.

questions.text: In ISO 27002:2022, control A.8.3 (Segregation of duties) is classified as a Technological control. What is the critical objective of implementing segregation of duties in information systems?
options: D. To replace the need for encryption and cryptographic controls
A. To prevent unauthorized or unintentional modifications by ensuring no single individual can complete critical transactions alone
C. To eliminate the need for background screening of employees
B. To reduce the number of system users and simplify user management
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.8.3 specifies that segregation of duties prevents unauthorized modifications by requiring multiple individuals for critical functions, reducing fraud and error risks through technical implementation in systems.
questions.justification_wrong: Segregation of duties does not reduce user count, eliminate screening requirements, or replace encryption. It specifically addresses transaction authorization and modification control through role-based access structures.

questions.text: Which combination of ISO 27002:2022 controls across different domains most effectively addresses comprehensive protection against ransomware and data loss incidents?
options: A. A.5.2 (Information security policies), A.6.3 (Awareness training), A.7.2 (Physical security perimeter), A.8.13 (Backup) and A.8.14 (Redundancy) working together
D. A.5.1 (Governance) control eliminating the need for backup and recovery mechanisms
C. A.7.1 (Physical security perimeters) exclusively protecting all data assets
B. A.8.13 (Backup) control implemented independently without organizational or people controls
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 requires integrated control implementation across all domains. A.5.2 establishes policies; A.6.3 builds awareness; A.7.2 secures facilities; A.8.13 and A.8.14 provide backup and redundancy. Together they provide defense-in-depth against ransomware.
questions.justification_wrong: Single controls cannot address ransomware comprehensively. Backup alone, physical controls alone, or governance alone lack the multi-layered approach required by ISO 27001:2022 for effective information security.

questions.text: An organization implements ISO 27001:2022 and must integrate A.5.1 (Policies for information security) with A.6.1 (Organization and management of information security). Which approach best ensures alignment between these organizational controls?
options: B. Create separate policy documents for each control to maintain independence and avoid redundancy in the ISMS.
D. Implement policies annually without management structure involvement to reduce administrative overhead.
A. Establish a governance framework where security policies define roles, responsibilities, and the organizational structure for security management through documented decision rights.
C. Assign all security responsibilities to the Chief Information Security Officer without hierarchical delegation.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 A.5.1 and A.6.1 require integrated governance where policies establish context and organizational structures implement them. Decision rights, roles, and management accountability must be clearly defined and linked across controls (Clause 5.1-5.3).
questions.justification_wrong: Separate policies create fragmentation (violates A.5.1 integration principle), exclusive CISOs bypass accountability, and annual cycles without structure fail to meet continual management requirements of A.6.1.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 audit, the auditor identifies that competence assessments (A.7.2 - People control) lack correlation with role-specific security tasks. How should the organization remediate this gap within the People domain?
options: C. Require all personnel to obtain CISSP certification regardless of job function.
D. Implement competence assessment only for IT security staff, excluding other departments.
B. Conduct annual generic security awareness training for all employees without role differentiation.

A. Map competence requirements to job roles using a RACI matrix, establish baseline skills per role, measure competence against security task requirements, and document training plans aligned to risk exposure.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 A.7.2 mandates competence evaluation against organizational needs. RACI mapping ensures role-task alignment, baseline skills establish measurable competence levels, and risk-based training addresses specific exposures per role (Clause 7.2).
questions.justification_wrong: Generic training ignores role-specific risks, blanket certifications waste resources and misalign with organizational context, and excluding non-IT staff violates A.7.2 universal applicability principle.

questions.text: An organization must design physical security controls (A.11.1 - Physical perimeter, A.11.2 - Physical entry) for a multi-site facility with varying risk levels. What is the most effective design approach aligned with ISO 27001:2022?
options: A. Conduct risk assessments per site identifying asset criticality, threat landscape, and occupancy patterns; implement tiered perimeter security (gates, surveillance, access controls) and entry protocols proportional to risk classification.
D. Rely exclusively on surveillance cameras without physical barriers or access control mechanisms.
B. Apply identical physical security measures across all sites to ensure consistency and simplify management.
C. Install maximum security controls at the main facility; treat branch offices with minimal controls.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 A.11.1-A.11.2 require risk-based, context-aware physical security. Site-specific risk assessments determine appropriate perimeter types (fences, gates) and entry controls (badges, biometrics) proportional to asset value and threat exposure (Clause 11.1-11.2).
questions.justification_wrong: Uniform controls ignore risk differentiation (violates proportionality principle), minimal branch office security creates vulnerabilities, and cameras alone lack preventive capability and violate defense-in-depth requirements.

questions.text: Within the Technological controls domain, an organization must synchronize A.5.15 (Access control), A.8.2 (Supply chain relationships), and A.8.3 (Information and communication technology supply chain) to prevent privilege escalation via vendor accounts. What is the integrated control strategy?
options: A. Establish vendor access policies with least privilege, multi-factor authentication, segregated accounts, activity monitoring, and contractual clauses mandating compliance with access control standards; conduct periodic access reviews and incident response protocols.
D. Implement access controls only for internal users; vendor access is the supplier's responsibility.
B. Grant vendors unlimited administrative access to reduce implementation friction and support delays.
C. Allow vendor accounts to inherit user permissions from shared pools to simplify account management.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 A.5.15, A.8.2, A.8.3 mandate integrated vendor access governance: least privilege (A.5.15), supply chain security agreements (A.8.2-A.8.3), monitoring (A.8.4), and contractual accountability. Segregated accounts with MFA and monitoring prevent unauthorized escalation (Clause 8.2-8.3).
questions.justification_wrong: Unlimited vendor access violates least privilege, shared permission pools create accountability gaps, and attributing vendor access solely to suppliers contradicts organizational accountability under ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5-6.

questions.text: An organization completing ISO 27001:2022 implementation must demonstrate control effectiveness across all four themes (Organizational-37, People-8, Physical-14, Technological-34). What is the most comprehensive evidence strategy for certification readiness?
options: B. Focus evidence collection exclusively on technological controls since they address the majority of risks.
C. Gather evidence only from high-risk areas identified in the initial risk assessment, excluding lower-risk domains.
D. Submit design documentation alone without operational evidence or testing results.

A. Develop a control evidence matrix mapping each control to design documentation, implementation artifacts (policies, logs, certificates), operational metrics, audit findings, and continuous improvement records; conduct internal audits and management reviews aligned to certification scope.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.4-4.7 require evidence demonstrating both control design and operational effectiveness across all themes. Evidence matrix ensures traceability; design + operational + audit evidence + continuous improvement demonstrates maturity (Clause 4.4, 8.1, 9.2-9.3).
questions.justification_wrong: Technological-only focus neglects organizational governance, people competence, and physical security interdependencies; risk-selective evidence gaps create audit non-conformities; design-only documentation fails operational effectiveness verification.

questions.text: An organization implementing ISO 27001:2022 control A.8.2 (Privileged access rights) discovers that 15% of user accounts have permanent administrative privileges, including several service accounts with domain admin rights that are not regularly reviewed. Database administrators have direct production access without session recording, and privileged account passwords are shared among team members. As Lead Implementer, which remediation action BEST aligns with ISO 27002:2022 guidance for this control?
options: A. Implement a Privileged Access Management (PAM) solution with just-in-time elevation, session monitoring, password vaulting, and establish a formal review process for privileged rights allocation with documented business justification

B. Reduce the number of privileged accounts by 50%, implement annual password changes for all administrative accounts, and require two-factor authentication only for external access to privileged systems

C. Create a privileged user acceptable use policy, mandate security awareness training for all administrators, and implement logging of privileged activities with quarterly manual reviews by department managers
D. Establish role-based access control (RBAC) for all users, implement single sign-on (SSO) across enterprise applications, and require privileged users to complete annual compliance attestations
questions.justification_correct: Option A comprehensively addresses ISO 27002:2022 control A.8.2 requirements by implementing technical controls (PAM with just-in-time/ephemeral privilege elevation, eliminating standing privileges), detective controls (session monitoring and recording), preventive controls (password vaulting to eliminate sharing), and governance controls (formal allocation and regular review processes with documented justification). This approach directly mitigates all identified issues: eliminates permanent admin privileges through JIT elevation, secures service accounts through vaulting, provides accountability through session recording, and prevents credential sharing.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B focuses on arbitrary reduction targets and inadequate password rotation (annual is insufficient for privileged accounts per ISO 27002:2022), while limiting MFA to external access only ignores internal threat vectors. Option C relies heavily on policy and awareness rather than technical enforcement, with quarterly manual reviews being insufficiently frequent and detective rather than preventive. Option D emphasizes general access control (RBAC/SSO) and compliance theater (attestations) but fails to address the specific privileged access issues: standing privileges, shared credentials, lack of monitoring, and insufficient review processes that A.8.2 specifically targets.
